check.command: awk -F: 'NR==FNR{uid[$1]=$3; shell[$1]=$7; next} ($1 in uid) && uid[$1]>=1000 && shell[$1] !~ /(nologin|false)$/ && ($5 > 60 || $5 <= 0) {print $1 " " $5}' /etc/passwd /etc/shadow

candidate_check.description: RHEL 8 user account passwords must be configured so that existing passwords are restricted to a 60-day maximum lifetime.

check_content_excerpt:
Check whether the maximum time period for existing passwords is restricted to 60 days with the following commands:

$ sudo awk -F: '$5 > 60 {print $1 " " $5}' /etc/shadow

$ sudo awk -F: '$5 <= 0 {print $1 " " $5}' /etc/shadow

If any results are returned that are not associated with a system account, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Configure non-compliant accounts to enforce a 60-day maximum password lifetime restriction.

$ sudo chage -M 60 [user]
title: RHEL 8 user account passwords must be configured so that existing passwords are restricted to a 60-day maximum lifetime.
